title: Indulgent Silky Canadian Cranberries Pilaf
description: This plant-based dish features dried cranberries and a blend of herbs, resulting in a silky and indulgent Canadian-inspired pilaf. The fermentation process adds a unique twist to this flavorful recipe.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the wild rice to the saucepan and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the vegetable broth, dried cranberries, thyme, and sage to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 40 minutes.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the pilaf to a baking dish and bake for 10-15 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
Remove the pilaf from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
